title: Cerastium bungeanum
intro: Cerastium bungeanum, also known as snow-in-summer, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its white, pink, or yellow flowers that bloom in the summer months. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Cerastium bungeanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cerastium bungeanum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Cerastium bungeanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is commonly known as Bunge's mouse-ear or Bunge's chickweed. This plant is a member of the genus Cerastium, which includes several other species of mouse-ear chickweeds. The Caryophyllaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that includes carnations, pinks, and stitchworts. Cerastium bungeanum is native to the mountainous regions of central Asia.
